result:
Good, confirmed with a real example. Here's the full answer.

## `struct list_head` value fields directly in `task_struct` (include/linux/sched.h, body lines 743–1554)

**Total: 14 fields.**

Unconditional (7, always present):
| Field | Line |
|---|---|
| `tasks` | 870 |
| `children` | 989 |
| `sibling` | 990 |
| `ptraced` | 999 |
| `ptrace_entry` | 1000 |
| `thread_group` | 1005 |
| `thread_node` | 1006 |

Inside `#ifdef` blocks (7, treated as present-but-conditional — counted in the total, config noted):
| Field | Line | Requires |
|---|---|---|
| `rcu_node_entry` | 847 | `CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU` |
| `rcu_tasks_holdout_list` | 856 | `CONFIG_TASKS_RCU` |
| `trc_holdout_list` | 863 | `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| `trc_blkd_node` | 864 | `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| `cg_list` | 1227 | `CONFIG_CGROUPS` |
| `pi_state_list` | 1238 | `CONFIG_FUTEX` |
| `perf_event_list` | 1246 | `CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS` |

**Unnamed struct/union handling:** `task_struct`'s body contains no anonymous `struct {}`/`union {}` blocks at all (verified by scanning lines 743–1554 for `struct {` / `union {` openings — none found), so there are no `list_head` fields to attribute to a nested anonymous aggregate; all 14 are directly members of `task_struct` itself.

**Exclusions checked but not counted:** `preempt_notifiers` (`struct hlist_head`, line 826), `kretprobe_instances`/`rethooks` (`struct llist_head`, lines 1509/1512), and `robust_list`/`compat_robust_list` (`struct robust_list_head *`, pointers) — different types, correctly excluded. No `struct list_head *` pointer-typed fields exist in the body.

## Real iteration example

`kernel/exit.c:1499`
```c
list_for_each_entry(p, &tsk->children, sibling) {
```
This walks `task_struct.children` (line 989) using the `sibling` field (line 990) as the `list_head` link, per the standard Linux parent/child list convention (`struct task_struct *p; list_for_each_entry(p, &tsk->children, sibling) { ... }`). Equivalent patterns appear at `kernel/exit.c:471`, `:479`, `:702`, and `kernel/fork.c:3223`.
